key_insight: LLM-ACES turns ODE discovery into a closed-loop process that uses an LLM to propose symbolic search regions and then actively gathers the most informative trajectories to resolve identifiability gaps.
engineering_relevance: This can improve data-efficient system identification for scientific and engineering dynamics, especially when experiments are expensive and limited observations make equations ambiguous.
method: LLM proposes operator priors to partition the symbolic search space.
Fit candidate ODEs within each region to current observations.
Use candidate disagreement to guide adaptive trajectory acquisition and iteratively refine the model.
evidence_signal: Evaluated on 122 ODE systems from ODEBench and ODEBase, the method reports the lowest median NMSE, strong symbolic accuracy (46.2% and 52.4%), and better performance with one-tenth the data.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality of adaptive data collection and LLM-generated priors, so poor acquisition choices or biased priors could still mislead the recovered equations.
paper_title: LLM-ACES: Closed-Loop Discovery of Dynamical Systems with LLM-Guided Adaptive Search
